channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Elevator door was 'broken' during Colorado mine tour accident that killed 1 and trapped 12
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Investigators on Friday were trying to figure out what led to an elevator accident inside a former Colorado gold mine that killed a tour guide, injured four others and left a separate group of 12 people trapped for hours at the bottom of the tourist attraction 1,000 feet (305 meters) b
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, AMY BETH HANSON, MEAD GRUVER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boeing will lay off 10% of its employees as a strike by factory workers cripples airplane production
item.description: Boeing plans to lay off about 10% of its workers in the coming months, about 17,000 people, as it continues to lose money and tries to deal with a strike that is crippling production of the company’s best-selling airline planes.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Allred pushes back on Cruz transgender attack ads
item.description: “Ted Cruz is lying again, but now he's lying about our children. I'm a dad. I'm also a Christian,” Allred said on camera.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: 7-Eleven to close over 400 locations in North America
item.description: Hundreds of “underperforming” 7-Eleven stores across North America are closing, the convenience store announced Thursday. Seven & I Holdings, 7-Eleven’s Japan-based parent company, revealed in its earnings report that 444 7-Eleven locations are closing.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: The attempted murder trial of the man charged with injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack in New York has been put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What's behind the northern lights that dazzled the sky farther south than normal
item.description: Another in a series of unusually strong solar storms hitting Earth produced stunning skies full of pinks, purples, greens and blues farther south than normal, including into parts of Germany, the United Kingdom, New England and New York City.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Montana businessman gets 2 years in prison for role in Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the US Capitol
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A Montana business owner and supporter of former President Donald Trump has been sentenced to two years in federal prison for his role in the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol that interrupted certification of the 2020 Electoral College vote.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Marathon to take place on Sunday
item.description: The Chicago Marathon will take place on Sunday, October 13, with thousands of runners and spectators expected to attend, and several downtown streets closures already in place.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis helps Waffle House employees cook for first responders
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis put on the gloves and picked up a spatula at a Sarasota-area Waffle House on Thursday to help cook for first responders after Hurricane Milton left a path of destruction across the Tampa Bay area.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This winter may be less predictable than the last few. Here's why
item.description: This winter was originally thought to be dominated by the La Niña phenomenon. But now, the Climate Prediction Center believes it will be a "weak and a short duration La Niña" – if it appears at all.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Abortion has passed inflation as the top election issue for women under 30, survey finds
item.description: A survey has found that abortion has passed inflation to become the top issue in the presidential election for women younger than 30.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Residents begin repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which killed at least 10
item.description: Florida residents began repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Blue cities plagued by homeless encampments face threat as woman’s body found in suitcase
item.description: After the remains of Shannon Reeder, 37, were found stuffed into a suitcase in a Seattle homeless encampment, experts say encampments nationwide need more government oversight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Box containing human head reportedly found on sidewalk of Chicago's West Side: report
item.description: Authorities in Chicago are investigating after a witness said they found a box on the sidewalk near the city's West Side that contained a human head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Israel agitators deface Harvard statute day after Oct. 7 anniversary: ‘Bring the war home’
item.description: Just after the one­-year anniversary of the Oct. 7 massacre in Israel, demonstrators vandalized Harvard's campus in "an act of solidarity with the Palestinian resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Penn State makes its first west coast Big Ten trip to USC
item.description: UNIVERSITY PARK (Nittany Nation) -- It's not your granddaddy's Big Ten anymore and this week's conference lineup is the people's exhibit A. Minnesota heads to UCLA, Ohio State is at Oregon, and Penn State heads to USC. Three games that hardly feel like Big Ten conference play.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Clay, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The musician and artist, whose real name is Brian Warner, has vehemently denied the allegations, though he has settled at least two related lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: 12 people trapped in mine successfully rescued
item.description: You can watch the full press conference in the video player above. UPDATE: THURSDAY 10/10/2024 8:02 p.m. (TELLER COUNTY, Colo.) — According to Teller County Sheriff Jason Mikesell, all 12 people who were trapped in the Mollie Kathleen Mine have been successfully rescued as of about 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Eberhardt, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa woman killed by falling branch after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Investigators said that it appears that reconstruction efforts were being done in the area when the branch fell on the victim. So far, the death appears to be an accident.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: A rare comet is expected to be visible to the naked eye across the Northern Hemisphere for several weeks in October.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, ABC 4

item.title: More than 500 people rescued from flooded Clearwater apartment complex after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Roadways across Pinellas County reopened Thursday morning after shutting down due to hazardous conditions caused by Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton weakens to Category 1, but danger remains in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday along Florida’s Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm, bringing powerful winds, deadly storm surge and potential flooding to much of the state. Milton drew fuel from exceedingly warm Gulf of Mexico waters, twice reaching Category 5 status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: In remote mountain communities cut off by Helene, residents look to the skies for aid
item.description: RAMSEYTOWN, N.C. (AP) — As the Black Hawk helicopter slowly descends in Ramseytown, North Carolina, a plume of sand kicks up. When the dust settles, the sprawling sea of stones and twisted metal beams becomes clear.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, ABC 4

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, widow of Robert F. Kennedy, hospitalized after suffering stroke, family says
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, is former President John F. Kennedy's sister-in-law and mother to 11 children, including former independent presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Hurricane Milton
item.description: Researchers on a Hurricane Hunter aircraft had a bump ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: An unusual hurricane season goes from ultra quiet to record busy and spawns Helene and Milton
item.description: Explosively intensifying Hurricane Milton is the latest freaky system to come out of what veteran hurricane scientists call the weirdest storm season of their lives.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
